extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won bunch sprint after Team Lotto NL-Jumbo controlled entire stage, never allowing break more than one minute gap
tactical_patterns: Lotto NL-Jumbo kept break under 60 seconds entire race on shortest stage, revealing high-speed control template for sprint stages.
DILLIER Silvan targeted intermediate sprint bonuses strategically, winning first sprint for GC time gains rather than stage positioning.
Late Quick-Step attack inside 10km served as lead-out disruption tactic for JAKOBSEN Fabio, not genuine breakaway attempt.

rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: Aggressively hunted intermediate sprint bonuses for GC time, replicating his 10th overall strategy from 2017 edition
rider_signals.HALLER Marco: neutral: Contested intermediate sprint after six-month injury layoff from broken kneecap, testing race fitness
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Clear positioning in finale but unable to match GROENEWEGEN's final acceleration despite Bora lead-out
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: neutral: Quick-Step's late attack gave armchair ride but positioning insufficient against GROENEWEGEN's speed
